commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But he shall say, I am no prophet, I am a farmer; for man taught me to keep cattle from my youth. Worldly occupations were incompatible with the office of prophets, 3 Kings xix 20. , and Amos vii. , and Matthew iv. The (Calmet) false (Haydock) prophets will become so odious, that people will excuse themselves from taking up the calling. I am condemned like him to labour, Genesis iii. Septuagint, "a man begot me. "Hebrew, "taught, or bought me, (Calmet) or caused me to work.
